1.texto_original: Disse-me, porém, o Senhor: Ainda que Moisés e Samuel se pusessem diante de mim, não poderia estar a minha alma com este povo. Lança-os de diante da minha face, e saiam eles.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremias 15:1

"Disse-me, porém, o Senhor: Ainda que Moisés e Samuel se pusessem diante de mim, não poderia estar a minha alma com este povo. Lança-os de diante da minha face, e saiam eles."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremias 15:1

Philological Analysis

"Disse-me, porém, o Senhor: Ainda que Moisés e Samuel se pusessem diante de mim, não poderia estar a minha alma com este povo. Lança-os de diante da minha face, e saiam eles."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

14.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremias 15:14

"E farei que sirvas os teus inimigos numa terra que não conheces; porque o fogo se acendeu em minha ira, e sobre vós arderá."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremias 15:14

Philological Analysis

"E farei que sirvas os teus inimigos numa terra que não conheces; porque o fogo se acendeu em minha ira, e sobre vós arderá."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
14.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"E farei que sirvas os teus inimigos numa terra que não conheces; porque o fogo se acendeu em minha ira, e sobre vós arderá."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Tu, ó Senhor, me conheces; lembra-te de mim, visita-me, e vinga-me dos meus perseguidores; não me arrebates, por tua longanimidade. Sabe que por amor de ti tenho sofrido afronta.

19.texto_original: Portanto assim diz o Senhor: Se tu voltares, então te restaurarei, para estares diante de mim; e se apartares o precioso do vil, serás como a minha boca; tornem-se eles a ti, mas não voltes tu a eles.
19.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremias 15:19

"Portanto assim diz o Senhor: Se tu voltares, então te restaurarei, para estares diante de mim; e se apartares o precioso do vil, serás como a minha boca; tornem-se eles a ti, mas não voltes tu a eles."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

20.texto_original: E eu te porei contra este povo como forte muro de bronze; eles pelejarão contra ti, mas não prevalecerão contra ti; porque eu sou contigo para te salvar, para te livrar, diz o Senhor.
20.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremias 15:20

"E eu te porei contra este povo como forte muro de bronze; eles pelejarão contra ti, mas não prevalecerão contra ti; porque eu sou contigo para te salvar, para te livrar, diz o Senhor."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
